records.ety: [F. concurrent, L. concurrens, p. pr. of concurrere.]

defs: Acting in conjunction; agreeing in the same act or opinion; contributing to the same event or effect; cooperating.
quotes: I join with these laws the personal presence of the kings' son, as a concurrent cause of this reformation.

defs: Conjoined; associate; concomitant; existing or happening at the same time.
quotes: There is no difference the concurrent echo and the iterant but the quickness or slowness of the return.
Changes . . . concurrent with the visual changes in the eye.

defs: Joint and equal in authority; taking cognizance of similar questions; operating on the same objects; as, the concurrent jurisdiction of courts.
One who, or that which, concurs; a joint or contributory cause.
quotes: To all affairs of importance there are three necessary concurrents . . . time, industry, and faculties.

defs: One pursuing the same course, or seeking the same objects; hence, a rival; an opponent.
quotes: Menander . . . had no concurrent in his time that came near unto him.

defs: One of the supernumerary days of the year over fifty-two complete weeks; -- so called because they concur with the solar cycle, the course of which they follow.
